title: polypodium struthionis
intro: Polypodium struthionis, also known as Lion's Tail or Ostrich Fern, is a type of fern found in the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique, long stolon-like stems and feathery fronds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polypodium struthionis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its rhizomes and roots are also used to make medicinal extracts.
